title: NIS2 Risk Analysis Evidence for Essential Entities

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIS2 Art.21(2) — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.21: NIS2 Risk Analysis Evidence for Essential Entities.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.21' running on index=risk sourcetype="stash", tenable:vuln, nis2_entity_classification.csv (entity_tier=essential), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Tenable Add-On for Splunk (Splunkbase 4060)
spl:
| inputlookup nis2_entity_classification.csv WHERE entity_tier="essential"
| rename asset_id as risk_object
| join risk_object [
    search index=risk sourcetype="stash" earliest=-30d
    | stats latest(risk_score) as residual_risk max(risk_score) as peak_risk by risk_object
]
| join risk_object [
    search index=tenable sourcetype="tenable:vuln" severity IN ("Critical","High") earliest=-14d
    | stats dc(plugin_id) as open_critical_high by host
    | rename host as risk_object
]
| eval analysis_stale=if(residual_risk > 80 OR open_critical_high > 5, "ESCALATE", "Monitor")
| table risk_object, business_service, residual_risk, peak_risk, open_critical_high, analysis_stale
| sort - residual_risk
description: Essential entities must implement a comprehensive set of risk-management measures proportionate to the risks posed. This use case aggregates residual risk scores, vulnerability exposure, and control maturity for assets tagged as essential — producing continuous evidence that formal risk analysis is refreshed and acted upon, not only performed annually on paper.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIS2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes risk, tenable and sourcetypes stash, tenable:vuln. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Tag all in-scope assets with `entity_tier` in `nis2_entity_classification.csv`; (2) ensure ES risk and vuln scans cover essential services; (3) define risk thresholds with CISO sign-off; (4) alert when residual risk or critical findings breach policy; (5) export quarterly risk analysis workbook attachments from dashboard PDFs. Indexes required: `index=risk`, `index=tenable`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=stash`, `sourcetype=tenable:vuln`.
visualization: Heatmap (risk by service), Table (essential assets breaching threshold), Single value (mean residual risk), Line chart (trend). Key fields: `analysis_stale`, `business_service`, `open_critical_high`, `peak_risk`, `residual_risk`, `risk_object`.
knownFalsePositives: Scanner findings that are false positives, accepted risks with a formal exception, or test assets outside the regulatory perimeter.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Tenable Add-On for Splunk (Splunkbase 4060).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=risk` `sourcetype="stash"`, `tenable:vuln`, `nis2_entity_classification.csv` (entity_tier=essential).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Tag all in-scope assets with `entity_tier` in `nis2_entity_classification.csv`; (2) ensure ES risk and vuln scans cover essential services; (3) define risk thresholds with CISO sign-off; (4) alert when residual risk or critical findings breach policy; (5) export quarterly risk analysis workbook attachments from dashboard PDFs.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_entity_classification.csv WHERE entity_tier="essential"
| rename asset_id as risk_object
| join risk_object [
    search index=risk sourcetype="stash" earliest=-30d
    | stats latest(risk_score) as residual_risk max(risk_score) as peak_risk by risk_object
]
| join risk_object [
    search index=tenable sourcetype="tenable:vuln" severity IN ("Critical","High") earliest=-14d
    | stats dc(plugin_id) as open_critical_high by host
    | rename host as risk_object
]
| eval analysis_stale=if(residual_risk > 80 OR open_critical_high > 5, "ESCALATE", "Monitor")
| table risk_object, business_service, residual_risk, peak_risk, open_critical_high, analysis_stale
| sort - residual_risk
```

Understanding this SPL

**NIS2 Risk Analysis Evidence for Essential Entities (Art. 21(2))** — Essential entities must implement a comprehensive set of risk-management measures proportionate to the risks posed. This use case aggregates residual risk scores, vulnerability exposure, and control maturity for assets tagged as essential — producing continuous evidence that formal risk analysis is refreshed and acted upon, not only performed annually on paper.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=risk` `sourcetype="stash"`, `tenable:vuln`, `nis2_entity_classification.csv` (entity_tier=essential). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Tenable Add-On for Splunk (Splunkbase 4060). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Renames fields with `rename` for clarity or joins.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **analysis_stale** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

This use case does not use a CIM tstats shortcut; the primary SPL above is the source of truth after field normalization in your environment.

Output fields: analysis_stale, business_service, open_critical_high, peak_risk, residual_risk, risk_object.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ES Risk framework views (index=risk) and the ServiceNow GRC console for the same period so risk scores and asset fields line up.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Heatmap (risk by service), Table (essential assets breaching threshold), Single value (mean residual risk), Line chart (trend).
grandmaExplanation: We essential entities must implement a comprehensive set of risk-management measures proportionate to the risks posed.
